---
title: "Perinatal Diet and Preeclampsia: Interactive Dashboard"
output: 
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: rows
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(here)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(DT)
library(htmltools)
library(reactable)

source(here("scripts", "lasso.R"))
source(here("scripts", "all_results.R"))
```

# Project Overview
=====================================

Row
-------------------------------------

### About this project
```{r}
HTML("
<div style='background-color:#f7f7f7; padding:15px; border-radius:10px;'>

<b>Background</b><br>
Preeclampsia is a hypertensive disorder of pregnancy and a leading cause of maternal and neonatal complications. Diet is a potentially modifiable factor that may influence risk, but evidence has been inconsistent when focusing on individual foods or nutrients.<br><br>

A dietary patterns approach considers overall eating behavior rather than single components, which may better capture real-world diets and their relationship with health outcomes.<br><br>

<b>What this dashboard does</b><br>
Identifies dietary patterns during pregnancy using dietary intake data (e.g., food group consumption) and multiple clustering methods, and evaluates their association with preeclampsia risk.<br><br>

<b>Results</b><br>
Presented as marginally standardized risk differences, reflecting population-average differences in risk between clusters.<br><br>

<b>Interpretation</b><br>
All estimates are scaled per <b>100 pregnancies</b> for interpretability.

</div>
")
```

# Clustering Variability in Dietary Patterns
=====================================

Column {.tabset}
-------------------------------------

### K-means
```{r}
tagList(
  div(style = "height:450px;",
      ggplotly(p1, tooltip = "text")
  ),
  
  div(
    style = "font-size:14px; line-height:1.5; margin-top:10px;",
    HTML("
<b>Summary</b><br>
    K-means identified clusters characterized by sweetened beverages, sweet bakery products, and white potatoes in cluster 1, and by vegetables (excluding potatoes), coffee and tea, and fruits in cluster 2.")
))
```

### K-medoids
```{r}
tagList(
  div(style = "height:450px;",
      ggplotly(p2, tooltip = "text")
  ),
  
  div(
    style = "font-size:14px; line-height:1.5; margin-top:10px;",
    HTML("
<b>Summary</b><br>
    K-medoids characterized cluster 1 by ready-to-eat cereal, crackers, and candy, and cluster 2 by alcoholic beverages, coffee and tea, and yogurt.")
))
```

### Hierarchical
```{r}
tagList(
  div(style = "height:450px;",
      ggplotly(p3, tooltip = "text")
  ),
  
  div(
    style = "font-size:14px; line-height:1.5; margin-top:10px;",
    HTML("
<b>Summary</b><br>
    For Hierarchical clustering, vegetables (excluding potatoes), coffee and tea, and diet beverages characterized cluster 1, whereas sweetened beverages, savory snacks, and pizza defined cluster 2.")
))
```

### GMM
```{r}
tagList(
  div(style = "height:450px;",
      ggplotly(p4, tooltip = "text")
  ),
  
  div(
    style = "font-size:14px; line-height:1.5; margin-top:10px;",
    HTML("
<b>Summary</b><br>
    In Gaussian Mixture Modelling, cluster 1 showed particularly elevated log-odds for sweetened beverages, 100% juice, and alcoholic beverages, while cluster 2's highest values were for diet beverages, beans/vegetables, and coffee and tea.")
))
```

# Perinatal Diet and Preeclampsia Risk
=====================================
Row {data-height=1}
-------------------------------------

```{r}
HTML("<h3>Risk Difference for Preeclampsia per <b>100 pregnancies</b></h3>")
```

Row {data-height=6}
-------------------------------------
```{r}
tagList(
  div(style = "height:450px;",
      ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")
  ),
  
  div(
    style = "background-color:white; 
            padding:12px; 
            border-radius:8px;
            font-size:14px; 
            line-height:1.6; 
            margin-top:10px;
            border: 1px solid #e0e0e0;",
))
```
Row {data-height=2}
-------------------------------------

```{r}
HTML("
<b>Summary</b><br>
K-means showed a lower risk of preeclampsia, while Hierarchical clustering showed a higher risk. K-medoids and GMM showed minimal differences. All confidence intervals included the null, suggesting no statistically significant associations.
")
```
